sections.heading: Surgery
sections.content:
Surgical resection is an option for a select subset of patients with very early-stage SCLC (clinical stage I–IIA, T1–2,N0,M0) after comprehensive staging. It is not a standard treatment for most SCLC patients due to the aggressive nature and early metastatic potential of the disease [1].

Principles:
- Stage I–IIA SCLC is diagnosed in <5% of patients with SCLC [1].
- Patients most likely to benefit are those with clinical stage I–IIA (T1–2,N0,M0) after standard staging evaluation [1].
- Prior to resection, all patients should undergo mediastinoscopy or other surgical mediastinal staging to rule out occult nodal disease [1].
- The preferred operation is lobectomy with mediastinal lymph node dissection or systematic lymph node sampling (e.g., ≥3 N2 and ≥1 N1 stations) [1].
- In patients who do not smoke, small lesions presumed to be SCLC on biopsy should be resected because they are likely carcinoids that have been misdiagnosed [1].
- Surgery may be considered for selected patients with T3 (based on size), N0 SCLC, if invasive mediastinal lymph node staging is negative [1].
- Intraoperative diagnosis of likely SCLC: perform mediastinal lymph node dissection or sampling with frozen section; if resectable, perform anatomic resection, preferably lobectomy. Pneumonectomy should not be performed if needed to encompass nodal metastatic disease [1].
- Patients who undergo complete resection should be treated with postoperative systemic therapy [1].

Resectability criteria: Surgical resection is considered for clinical stage I–IIA (T1–2,N0,M0) SCLC with negative pathologic mediastinal staging. For T3 (size) N0, surgery may be considered if invasive mediastinal staging is negative. Pneumonectomy is not recommended if needed to encompass nodal metastatic disease [1].

Pre-operative optimisation: Multidisciplinary evaluation is recommended before any surgery. Pulmonary function tests (PFTs) should be performed during evaluation for surgery. Patients who do not smoke and have small lesions presumed to be SCLC should undergo resection to rule out carcinoid misdiagnosis [1].

cards.name: Lobectomy with mediastinal lymph node dissection or systematic lymph node sampling
cards.detail:
Approach: Open or minimally invasive (thoracoscopic). Systematic sampling should include ≥3 N2 and ≥1 N1 stations [1].
Indications: Clinical stage I–IIA (T1–2,N0,M0) SCLC after negative pathologic mediastinal staging [1].
Key complications: Standard surgical risks: bleeding, infection, pneumothorax, prolonged air leak, bronchopleural fistula, arrhythmia, and mortality [1].
Operative mortality: Not specified in source; lobectomy mortality is generally low (1–3%) in high-volume centers.
